---
title: "Build the Lab"
description: "Notes on reproducible research environments and experimental pipelines"
lang: en
page-layout: article
sidebar: pysdm-en
---

Good numerical experiments begin with a good environment. Dependencies, input data, configuration files, automated validation, and figure generation are documented as reproducible units.

::: {.callout-note}
The implementation code for this track is connected to the [PySDM-Seeding-Lab repository](https://github.com/Jinu219/PySDM-Seeding-Lab).
:::

These notes focus on the problem behind each change, the design decision, the failure that challenged it, the evidence used for validation, and the limits that remain.

::: {.manual-post-list}
::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-07-16 · Jinu</div>

### [Qualifying Rain-Producing Experiments and Letting the Server Work Overnight](posts/build-the-lab/2026-07-16-rain-server-execution/index.qmd)

Separating converged absolute rain state from unconverged seeding response, then adding common-seed evidence, process isolation, detached jobs, and bounded server parallelism.
:::

::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-07-14 · Jinu</div>

### [From Code That Produces Results to Code That Makes Them Trustworthy](posts/build-the-lab/2026-07-14-research-quality/index.qmd)

Adding diagnostic provenance, native PySDM outputs, numerical qualification, water-budget gates, spectrum transitions, and reproducible reports.
:::

::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-07-13 · Jinu</div>

### [Turning PySDM Experiment Scripts into a Seeding Lab](posts/build-the-lab/2026-07-13-scenario-workflow/index.qmd)

Reframing manual scripts as a Scenario → Sweep → Run → Dashboard experimental workflow.
:::
:::
